pi_items.source: Task description step 4 specifies exact file path
Task description step 5 specifies exact file path
Task description explicitly states 'rectangle 80 mm wide'
Task description explicitly states '60 mm tall'
Task description explicitly states 'Four holes with 3.2 mm diameter'. This is a standard size for M3 fasteners (verified via PCB manufacturing standards)
Mathematically derived: diameter (3.2) / 2 = 1.6. Task description confirms '1.6 mm radius'
Task description states '4 mm inset from each corner'. Verified mathematically: (4,4) is 4mm from (0,0); (76,4) is 80-4=76, 4mm from (80,0); (76,56) is 80-4=76, 60-4=56; (4,56) is 4, 60-4=56
Reasonable tolerance for PCB manufacturing. Standard PCB precision is ±0.05mm; 0.5mm is appropriately generous for this task. Verified in verifier.py line 60
Reasonable radius tolerance for PCB drilling (approximately 3% of hole diameter). Verified in verifier.py line 61

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic PCB design task with well-defined specifications. The task requires creating a 2D PCB board outline (80mm × 60mm rectangle) with four mounting holes (3.2mm diameter, positioned 4mm inset from each corner) in FreeCAD and exporting to DXF format. All metadata values are internally consistent, mathematically correct, and aligned with industry standards for PCB manufacturing. Board dimensions are within typical custom PCB size ranges (51-254mm). The 3.2mm hole diameter is standard for M3 fasteners. Tolerance values (0.5mm position, 0.1mm radius) are reasonable for PCB work, with position tolerance being generous relative to standard ±0.05mm PCB precision.
